Of course. Here is the solution based on the horizontal line test.

**Understanding the Horizontal Line Test**

The horizontal line test is a visual way to determine if a function is **one-to-one**. A function is one-to-one if every horizontal line you can draw intersects the graph of the function at **most once**.

*   If any horizontal line intersects the graph more than once, the function is **not** one-to-one.
*   If every horizontal line intersects the graph only once (or not at all), the function **is** one-to-one.

**Applying the Test to the Graph**

1.  Imagine drawing a horizontal line across the graph, for example, at y = 1. This line intersects the curve at exactly one point.
2.  Now, imagine drawing another horizontal line at y = -1. This line also intersects the curve at exactly one point.
3.  You can visually check any other horizontal line (e.g., y = 0, y = 1.5, y = -0.5). No matter where you draw a horizontal line, it will only ever cross the graphed curve a single time.

**Conclusion**

Since any horizontal line drawn on the graph intersects the curve at most once, the function represented by the graph **is one-to-one**.

Question: $$f ( x ) = x^{2} - 4 , x \geq 0$$
Answer: a. $$f^{-1} ( x ) = \sqrt{x + 4}$$ b. Domain $$\text{:} x \geq -4 , \text{range} \text{:} y \geq 0$$

 f⁻¹(x) = √(x + 4)

b.  Domain and range of the inverse

 f maps [0, ∞) to [–4, ∞) (since the smallest value occurs at x = 0).

 Original domain is [0, ∞), so Range( f⁻¹ ) = [0, ∞).

b)  Domain = [–4, ∞); Range = [0, ∞)
